opps.title: ISBEE/IEE Sources Sought for Revenue Enhancement Services for the IHS Billings Area
opps.description:
SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE

This is a Sources Sought notice. This is NOT a solicitation for proposals, proposal abstracts, or quotations. Pursuant to 48 CFR Part 326, Subpart 326.603-3 and IHM 5.5-6, Indian Health Service must use the negotiation authority of the Buy Indian Act, 25 U.S.C. 47, to give preference to Indian Economic Enterprises (IEE) whenever the use of that authority is authorized and practicable.

The Government is seeking to identify Indian Small Business Economic Enterprises (ISBEEs), Indian Economic Enterprises (IEE), and any other category of vendor capable of performing the below Indian Health Services (IHS) requirement. Any vendor responding to this notice should submit their capability statement as well as the Buy Indian Act Indian Economic Enterprise Representation Form (attached).

Consistent with the revised Buy Indian Act, HHS/IHS adopted the definition of an Indian Economic Enterprise (IEE) set forth in 48 CFR 1480.201.  This definition includes the following requirement, amongst others: “the Indians or Indian Tribes must, together, receive at least a majority of the earnings from the contract.”. 

Instructions for submission are outlined further below.

DEFINITIONS

INDIAN ECONOMIC ENTERPRISE (IEE):

Any business activity owned by one or more Indians, Federally Recognized Indian Tribes, or Alaska Native Corporations provided that:


	The combined Indian, Federally Recognized Indian Tribe or Alaska Native Corporation ownership of the enterprise constitutes not less than 51%;
	The Indian, Federal Recognized Indian Tribes, or Alaska Native Corporations must, together, receive at least 51% of the earnings from the contract; and
	The management and daily business operations must be controlled by one or more individuals who are Indians.


DESCRIPTION OF REQUIREMENT

Revenue Enhancement Services

NAICS:

518210 - Computing Infrastructure Providers, Data Processing, Web Hosting, and Related Services

Unique Requirements:

SEE ATTACHMENT:

NO SET ASIDE IS TO BE USED FOR THIS SOLE SOURCE REQUIREMENT. THE ASSOCIATED NAICS CODE IS 541511, CUSTOM COMPUTER PROGRAMMING SERVICES.



***THE SOLE SOURCE DETERMINATION IS BASED ON NOAA’S REQUIREMENT THAT SAINT LOUIS UNIVERSITY (SLU) IS THE ONLY VENDOR THAT CAN PROVIDE THIS SERVICE.

SAINT LOUIS UNIVERSITY (SLU), POSSESSES THE UNIQUE TECHNICAL EXPERTISE AND EXISTING SOFTWARE INFRASTRUCTURE REQUIRED TO MEET THE AGENCY'S NEEDS. SLU HAS ALREADY DEVELOPED THE PROTOTYPE PRECISE-CLOCK-ESTIMATION FRAMEWORK SPECIFICALLY DESIGNED TO INTERFACE WITH NGS’S PROPRIETARY MPAGES LIBRARIES. THIS FRAMEWORK WAS THE BASIS FOR PEER-REVIEWED RESEARCH PROVING THAT NGS CAN ACHIEVE CLOCK-PRODUCT PERFORMANCE COMPARABLE TO THE INTERNATIONAL GNSS SERVICE (IGS). THE PROJECT REQUIRES IN-DEPTH EXPERTISE IN GNSS DATA PROCESSING, CLOCK ANALYSIS, AND OPTIMAL ESTIMATION TECHNIQUES THAT ARE HIGHLY SPECIALIZED. SLU IS UNIQUELY POSITIONED DUE TO THEIR INTIMATE FAMILIARITY WITH THE EXISTING MATLAB AND C++ FRAMEWORK THAT MUST NOW BE MODERNIZED AND MIGRATED TO A STANDARD LINUX ENVIRONMENT. SLU INCLUDES STAFF ON THE IGS CLOCK WORKING GROUP. THIS SPECIFIC LEADERSHIP FUNCTION IS CRITICAL FOR THIS PROJECT, WHICH REQUIRES COORDINATING INDEPENDENT VALIDATION AND PERFORMANCE ASSESSMENTS OF NGS CLOCK PRODUCTS WITH THE IGS. SLU HAS ALREADY ESTABLISHED A DEDICATED GNSS STATION AND TESTING ENVIRONMENT SPECIFICALLY FOR THE EVALUATION OF THE SAFRAN SRO-5680 ATOMIC CLOCKS PROVIDED BY NGS. THIS EXISTING SETUP ALLOWS FOR IMMEDIATE COMMENCEMENT OF THE FREQUENCY STABILITY AND ENVIRONME

Provide services in accordance with the attached Statement of Work. The primary goal of establishing this contract is for electronic security consulting services to support the procurement and design phases of the U.S. Marshals Service (USMS) electronic security system project for Courthouses in Hartford, CT, and Chattanooga, TN.

Please read the attached documents for information on the scope of work and additional information included. The consulting services outlined focus on supporting GSA through scope development, design review, procurement, and initial security contractor onboarding for new electronic security systems (Access Control, Video Surveillance, Intrusion Detection, and Intercom/Emergency Communication). Services will primarily be performed remotely, with necessary on-site activities at Washington, D.C., Atlanta, GA, Hartford, CT, and Chattanooga, TN. The estimated duration for these services is 2-years from the commencement date, with implementation support projected from August 3, 2026, to August 3, 2028.

The Government intends to award an order for services in accordance with FAR 8.4 (GSA Class Deviation RFO-2025-08) on the basis of best value to the Government. All evaluation factors other than cost or price, when combined, are approximately equal to price. 

The technical factors that will be evaluated are the offerors Past Experience and Key Personnel, and will be of equal weight when evaluating the proposals.

Past Experience

The Offeror shall demonstrate past experience designing, installing, integrating, testing, and commissioning electronic security systems for facilities of similar size, scope, and complexity. Relevant systems include access control, video surveillance, intrusion detection, duress alarms, intercom systems, alarm monitoring, and related security infrastructure.

The ARNG G-9 Directorate (ARNG-IEZ) manages installation programs to support readiness and mission execution by providing policy guidance, resources to create, sustain, and operate facilities, and resource accountability to the Director, ARNG, and Congress. This task order will support ARNG G-9's mission through Sustainment, Restoration, and Modernization (SRM); Base Operations Support (BOS); Sustainability; and Military Construction (MILCON), as well as training for State Project and Energy Managers, Construction and Facility Management Officers (CFMOs), and preparation of the Annual Journal to inform Congress and the 54 States and Territories. Key requirements include Real Estate Support, Sustainability and Energy Program Support, Strategic Communications and Training Management, Budget and Program Planning and Analysis, MILCON Budget Execution Analysis, Facility Operations Support, and Annual Journal Preparation. The task order deliverables will cover all 50 states, three territories, and the District of Columbia, enabling ARNG G-9 to acquire and distribute resources for operations and maintenance of ARNG facilities.

	Description.
	
		The commercial product requirement is for no less than four (4) unit seats of subscriptions and licenses that include and provide access to:
		
			Commercial service to visualize location-based data via a browser-based platform (tied to individual PKI certificates)
			Training materials and documentation
			Ongoing platform and open-source data offering upgrades
		
		
		Query access to a variety of open-source data sources, specifically including the GDELT dataset, supporting a minimum of 200 queries per user per month, which includes:
		
			Turnkey solution to analyze, join, and geographically map event data from the GDELT dataset
			Support a "Bring Your Own Data" (BYOD) capability, allowing users to import up to 100 GB of user-controlled data per seat in CSV, KML, and GeoJSON formats
			All data access shall be restricted to use within the commercial instance and shall permit the creation of derivative products
		
		
		Access to a rolling three (3) years of historical, global AdTech data, providing location and time-stamp reporting for mobile devices (with data latency ranging from hours up to 3-day delay), which includes:
		
			All mobile AdID shall be anonymized and normalized to ensure privacy compliance
			Integrated layered data sources, including ADS-B and satellite AIS for aircraft and maritime detection, supported by scalable machine learning capabilities

opps.title: AN/TMQ-53 Tactical Meteorological Observing System (TMOS) Contractor Logistics Support (CLS)
opps.description:
This RFP has been Amended with the following changes. Due date extended to 20 July 2026; added the Questions and Answers, PWS Rev03, A022 CDRL attachment, Section L Rev02, and Solicitation Amendment 0001. All other terms, conditions, and requirements remain unchanged.

The Air Force Life Cycle Management Center (AFLCMC), Aerospace Management Systems Division, Weather Systems Branch (AFLCMC/ESAK), Hanscom AFB, MA 01731, is issuing a formal Request for Proposal (RFP) to provide Tactical Meteorological Observing System (TMOS) Contractor Logistics Support (CLS) for sustainment efforts supporting TMQ-53 systems from FY26 to FY31.

The contractor shall provide worldwide Contractor Logistics Support (CLS) to sustain currently fielded TMQ-53 systems in their baseline configuration. This effort encompasses comprehensive program management, logistics support (including helpdesk, maintenance, and spares/repair management), Operational, Safety, Suitability, and Effectiveness (OSS&E) impact analysis, test and evaluation, Configuration Control Board (CCB) support, and obsolescence and systems engineering. Additionally, the contractor is responsible for tracking and reporting critical system performance metrics (such as System Availability, Mission Capable, and MTBF rates), managing program data (including GFM-A, materials, shipping, and travel), and providing computer resource support, Engineering Change Proposals (ECPs), cybersecurity, technical manual maintenance, and system upgrades due to technical refresh. While worldwide support is required, there is no requirement for contractor travel to overseas locations.

This proposed effort is a total small business set-aside. The Government has determined, IAW FAR Subpart 19.103, that the NAICS classification of 541990 - All Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services best describes the principal nature of the product and service being acquired and represents the greatest percentage of the contract price. This classif

opps.title: Google Workspace Enterprise Plus
opps.description:
This procurement, supporting the Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO), is for the use of Google Workspace Enterprise Plus Apps to support CISAâ€™s IT Operations performance functions. The Enterprise Plus apps are a cloud-based browser with built-in controls that provide encryption and verification which enables work from anywhere and eliminates the need for VPNs. The apps have the ability to operate on a global scale while protecting information from phishing, malware, ransomware, and supply chain attacks. And the apps have secure endpoints that do not require patching and strong account takeover protections.

The software will be used to analyze CISA datasets to enhance leadership awareness of trends and organizational performance. The software procured must be capable of conducting real- time analysis; be relatively easy to use; allow data to flow easily into and out of the software; have forecasting capabilities that can predict values based on trends in the dataset; utilize user- friendly dashboards to format visualizations; and support professional reporting formats and data visualizations suitable for executive-level presentations.

 The contractor shall provide the CISA Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) with the following: 
1. Google Workspace Enterprise Plus; New â€“ Quantity: 1,300, 
2. Google Workspace Enterprise Plus; Archived â€“ Quantity: 1,300, 
3. G Suite Support Base Fee; - Quantity: 1, 
4. G Suite Support Variable Fee; - Quantity: 1,
5. Cloud Platform â€“ Support G Suite; Quantity: 50,000

opps.description:
NATURE OF REQUIREMENT:

Request for Quote, Security Card Reader System Replacement

In accordance with Federal Supply Schedules Contracts, please submit a quote on the above-mentioned project in accordance with the attached documents. 

DELIVERY AND PERFORMANCE PERIOD REQUIREMENTS

Delivery Date:
The total period of performance for this task order is 30 days after notice to proceed. 

Place of Performance:
All work shall be performed at the following location:
Robert A. Young (RAY) Federal Building 
1222 Spruce Street 
St. Louis, MO 63103

PROPOSAL SUBMISSIONS

Proposals shall consist of the following documents, completed and executed in accordance with this Solicitation:

Price Proposal

The Price Proposal shall consist  a detailed cost breakout. The cost break down shall sum to the total cost of the price proposed for the project. The price proposal acceptance period shall be 90 days from the request for proposal due date.

Deadline for Submissions:

Provide a proposal on or before July 23, at 3:00 p.m. Central Standard Time.  

Submit proposals electronically to the Contracting Officer at: stephan.harris@gsa.gov or uploaded to the GSA eBbuy Website.

Electronic Submission of Proposals

Offers sent shall be deemed timely if received by the email address of the GSA Point of Contact (POC) listed herein on or before the date and time established for receipt of offers, as recorded by the Government computer system's documentation of the exact official time of receipt, not by the time it was sent by the Offeror or received on the GSA server. It is the Offeror's responsibility to ensure proposals are complete and submitted early enough to be received by the GSA POC before the deadline, and to confirm receipt before the deadline. Proposals or portions of proposals which are received by the GSA POC later than the time noted within will not be accepted, reviewed, or considered.

NOTE: The maximum file size is 25 MB, therefore contractors/offerors need to plan accordingly.

The computer software reservation system must provide a full range of travel services necessary to support the government at multiple facilities.

The anticipate period of performance is for a base one-year period, with four (4) one-year periods.

opps.description:
This is a pre-solicitation notice of a proposed contract action issued in accordance with part 12 – Acquisition of Commercial Products and Commercial Services. This is not a solicitation or invitation for offerors to submit bids.

The Federal Bureau of Prisons (FBOP), located at 320 First St. NW, Washington DC, anticipates issuance of request for quotation (RFQ) 15BPCC26Q00000020 for a single award firm-fixed price (FFP) definitive contract for electronic legal research to be utilized all Bureau of Prisons institutions throughout the United States.

The performance period will include a one-year base followed by one option period with an Option to Extend Services FAR 52.217-8.

This acquisition is not set-aside for small business concerns. This will be solicited open market on an unrestricted basis. The NAICS code for this requirement is 519290 – Web Search Portals and All Other Information Services and PSC Code R605 – Library Services.

A solicitation will be issued on or about March 19, 2026, and will be posted to SAM.gov, with an estimated closing date of March 26, 2026. Hard copies of the solicitation will not be made available.  The website provides download instructions.  Any secure/locked documents (if applicable) uploaded with the solicitation will require an active Sam.gov vendor registration for access.  If you encounter problems accessing these documents, please contact the Federal Service Desk at 866-606-8220.  All future information about this solicitation, including any amendments, will also be distributed solely through this website.  Interested parties are responsible for continuously monitoring this website to ensure that they have the most recent information about this proposed contract action.

IMPORTANT: Any contractor interested in obtaining a contract award with the Federal Bureau of Prisons must be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM) at www.sam.gov. All offerors are urged to double check their SAM registration

opps.title: Presolicitation Notice: Publicly Available Information (PAI) Alerting Service
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
UPDATE 3 APRIL 2026

The purpose of this update is to remove the restriction of foreign owned vendors from competing for this requirement previously announced on 30 March 2026. There is no restriction on foreign vendors.

*****************************************************************************************

UPDATE 26 MARCH 2026

opps.title: Illegal Passing of School Bus Violation Enforcement in the Court Sys & Model Law
opps.description:
As stated in the recently completed State Laws Digest for Illegal Passing of School Buses, the term "illegal passing" is a prevalent concept; however, the report illustrated its lack of universality. Variations in state laws dictate that passing a bus from the front on a divided highway may be illegal in some states but permissible under specific roadway conditions in others. What constitutes illegal behavior in one jurisdiction may not be prohibited in a neighboring one. Given the high mobility of the U.S. population, the proximity of urban centers to multiple jurisdictions, and the significant disparities in the content and language of applicable Vehicle and Traffic Laws (VTL), confusion regarding the legality of actions surrounding a school bus is not unexpected.

The lack of clarity in drafting illegal passing provisions further compromises the ability of the typical motorist to understand these VTL requirements. Some jurisdictions have redrafted their amended provisions to include, among other things, the use of cameras. Thus, their updates present a mostly coherent, integrated statement of the law. Other jurisdictions have provisions that appear essentially sequential in design. New or changed provisions simply appear at the end of the existing VTL section, even if they logically relate to a topic much earlier in the material. This type of statutory construction is difficult to follow and can be misleading.

As recommended in the state laws digest, and in anticipation of future legislative interest, this project will focus more deeply on current illegal passing laws and develop a draft model state law to address incidents of illegal passing of stopped school buses. This report will also investigate the potential benefits and feasibility of the citation being deemed a moving violation.

This project will review the current research and practice of enforcement of the law against illegal passing of school buses. This project will provide if a model law would be n
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is issuing this solicitation to address the growing concern for student safety on and around school buses. The project aims to develop a model law for the illegal passing of school buses, which varies significantly across jurisdictions, leading to confusion and inconsistent enforcement. The initiative is prompted by alarming statistics indicating that illegal passing incidents result in serious injuries and fatalities among children.
